working_on: Cleanup of misleading S-0079 artifacts. S-0079 produced two valid deliverables (B1 audit script + audit report) and two misleading deliverables (B3 recovery executor + user-driven recovery guide) — both encoded the wrong recovery shape (separate session reads transcripts and writes drawers). The user's actual ask was a brief prompt template they could give to ORIGINAL sessions still accessible to them, so each original session writes its own MemPalace entries in its own voice (no impersonation; honest attribution; original session's own context). Delete the misleading files; correct STATE.md + ENGINE_LOG.md + S-0079 archive narrative; update audit report to point at the correct recovery shape (which is just a brief reflection prompt the user gives to each original session — no tooling needed beyond the missing-session list).
declared_scope: Delete engine/docs/audits/mempalace-recovery-guide-user-driven.md (encodes wrong recovery direction). Delete engine/tools/recover_mempalace_from_transcript.py + engine/tools/test_recover_mempalace_from_transcript.py (wrapper's --prompt mode + path resolution are unnecessary; original sessions write for themselves). Edit engine/session/archive/S-0079.json outcome_summary + scope_delivery to add correction notes flagging the misdirection. Edit STATE.md Last build session row to reflect what was actually valid. Edit ENGINE_LOG.md [Unreleased] section to mark S-0079 B3/guide entries as withdrawn-and-corrected and add the cleanup entry. Update engine/docs/audits/mempalace-attribution-S0001-to-S0077.md with the actual recovery shape — a brief prompt template the user gives to each original session, sourced from the chat where the prompt landed. The audit script + audit report stay (correct work).
outcome_summary: Cleanup session correcting S-0079's misdirection. S-0079 produced one valid deliverable (B1 audit script + audit report at engine/docs/audits/mempalace-attribution-S0001-to-S0077.md) and two misdirected deliverables (B3 wrapper engine/tools/recover_mempalace_from_transcript.py + recovery guide engine/docs/audits/mempalace-recovery-guide-user-driven.md). Both misdirected artifacts encoded the wrong recovery shape — a SEPARATE session reads transcripts and writes drawers about another session, first as subagent first-person ventriloquism (rejected by harness denial during S-0067 dry-run), then as analytical-voice authorship from a separate session (still wrong-shaped). The user's actual ask was a brief reflection prompt to paste into ORIGINAL sessions still accessible to the user; the original session writes its own MemPalace entries in its own voice (honest first-person, attributed to its own S-NNNN, original session's own context — no transcript re-reading, no separate session). S-0080 deleted the wrong-shaped artifacts (wrapper + tests + guide), added a corrective 'Recovery shape (corrected at S-0080)' section to the audit report explaining the actual shape, edited engine/session/archive/S-0079.json outcome_summary + scope_delivery to embed [CORRECTION (S-0080)] notes inline so future readers see the correction immediately, replaced STATE.md Last build session with S-0080 narrative + added a superseded-narrative row for S-0079, marked the S-0079 ENGINE_LOG entries with strikethrough and added Removed/Changed/Added sections for the cleanup. The 6 S-0067 dry-run mempalace items rolled back at S-0079 stay rolled back (the rollback was correct; only the followup direction was wrong). The B1 audit script + audit report are the only S-0079 deliverables that survive S-0080's correction. Two-commit pattern (eager-claim aa9da3b + close commit). Lesson captured in S-0080 diary write: when an approved plan frames a problem in a particular shape ('recovery via subagent transcript-crawl'), the framing carries assumptions that may not match the user's actual constraints; each new constraint should re-open the framing, not just narrow the implementation within it. Forward state: S-0081 = OQ-DEC1-A/C/D settlement (only remaining Phase 6 master plan prereq); Phase 6 master plan authoring shifts to S-0082+; MemPalace recovery is user-driven via the brief reflection prompt the user pastes into each missing session per the audit's missing-session list.

scope_delivery.explanation: Yes. Declared cleanup scope landed in full: deleted the three misleading files (wrapper, wrapper tests, recovery guide); added the corrective Recovery shape section to the audit report; embedded [CORRECTION (S-0080)] notes inline in S-0079 archive outcome_summary + scope_delivery; replaced STATE.md Last build session with S-0080 narrative and added a superseded-narrative row for S-0079; marked S-0079 ENGINE_LOG entries with strikethrough and added Removed/Changed/Added sections; bumped Next session work item to S-0081. The audit trail now honestly reflects what S-0079 delivered (B1 audit + report) vs what was misdirected (B3 wrapper + recovery guide). No descope; no reorder; no in-flight scope expansion.
